lesson: c3-l03 / AI for Practice and Assessment
title: AI for Practice and Assessment

scenes.name: Title
scenes.text: AI for practice and assessment. [pause] Turning every mistake into a learning opportunity.

scenes.name: Shift
scenes.text: From testing to learning through practice. [pause] AI does not just generate tasks. It combines three things that are hard to do at scale — immediate feedback, adaptive difficulty, and endless practice material.

scenes.name: Use cases
scenes.text: What AI practice systems look like in the classroom. [pause] Quizzes. Drills. Interactive dialogues. Adaptive problem sets. [pause] Every one of them — personalised to the student.

scenes.name: Loop
scenes.text: The practice loop. [pause] AI generates a problem. The student solves it. The AI evaluates and explains. [pause] If correct — next, harder. If wrong — a similar problem and a clear correction.

scenes.name: Feedback
scenes.text: Feedback is where the value lives. [pause] A worksheet says right or wrong. [pause] AI explains the error, names the misconception, and generates targeted practice. [pause] Every mistake becomes information.

scenes.name: Adaptive
scenes.text: Adaptive difficulty keeps students in the zone of growth. [pause] Struggling? It eases back. Confident? It pushes harder. [pause] Personalised pacing — for every student, at any time.

scenes.name: Misuse
scenes.text: But practice can be misused. [pause] The line between 'help me work' and 'do the work for me' is the line teachers must teach.

scenes.name: Teacher
scenes.text: Help students design their practice. [pause] Generic prompts give generic practice. [pause] Specific prompts — by topic, level, and goal — produce learning. And remind them: verify the feedback. AI is not infallible.

scenes.name: Closing
scenes.text: Practice with purpose. [pause] Feedback with insight.
